title: Error adding build tags
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** I am new to Podman, and am attempting to build the latest version on a Debian 11 VM by following the instructions at [https://podman.io/getting-started/installation](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation)

At the `make BUILDTAGS=` step, regardless of the options used (e.g. `"seccomp apparmor"` or `""`) the following error is generated: ``` vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runc/libcontainer/cgroups/getallpids.go:4:2: cannot find package "." in: /home//podman/vendor/io/fs make: *** [Makefile:309: bin/podman] Error 1 ``` **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Follow build instructions at [https://podman.io/getting-started/installation](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation) 2. During 'Get Source Code' phase, run `make BUILDTAGS="seccomp apparmor"` or `make BUILDTAGS=""` **Describe the results you received:** ``` $ make BUILDTAGS="seccomp apparmor" Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag. Install libsystemd on Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based distro for journald support. CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build \ -mod=vendor \ -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.gitCommit=5e2fcf813d54980df00976a01fca571b7007ae16 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.buildInfo=1642620983 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc ' \ -tags "seccomp apparmor" \ -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runc/libcontainer/cgroups/getallpids.go:4:2: cannot find package "." in: /home//podman/vendor/io/fs make: *** [Makefile:309: bin/podman] Error 1 ``` **Describe the results you expected:** Successfully add build tags **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** All dependencies in the instructions are installed and verified Go version is `go1.15.15 linux/amd64` **Output of `podman version`:** ``` N/A ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` N/A ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` N/A ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Your version of `go` is probably too old, since `io/fs` was added in go1.16

The system go compiler in Debian 11 was [golang-go](https://packages.debian.org/search?keywords=golang-go) version 1.15 (too old)...
Yeah I think we require 1.16 now.

Docs say 1.12, but [go.mod](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/go.mod#L3) says 1.16

> https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#building-missing-dependencies
>
> Be careful to double-check that the version of golang is new enough (i.e. go version), version 1.12.x or higher is supported. 

Maybe a quick `sed` there, perhaps ?
The version in go.mod is generated by go itself so this should always be the correct one
Concur that we need to update the installation page. Maybe change it from a static version to a link to the specific line of `go.mod`? That might be confusing for users, though...
Just confirming that upgrading the go version resolved my issue. Many thanks.
